items.name: Pretext Layout Lab
items.subtitle: Text-aware UI cards without DOM reflow
items.note: A browser text-measurement lab that predicts multiline card height, line count, and overflow risk before layout. Built to understand how high-performance UI systems avoid expensive DOM measurement and keep responsive cards stable.

proof.problem: Browser DOM measurement forces layout reflow — expensive when cards, lists, or dashboards need to know text size before render.
proof.concept: prepare() once, then layout() with pure arithmetic when width or line-height changes.
proof.why: Stable cards, honest overflow warnings, and virtualization-friendly height prediction without thrashing the layout tree.
edgeCases: Long unbroken tokens and code-like strings
Line budgets for title vs body
proves: I know why DOM measurement can be expensive.
I can predict line count and height before rendering.
I can use that for real UI systems: cards, chat, dashboards, Kanban boards.
proof.meta: This site's own project cards use the same measurement idea: prepare text once, calculate layout against card width, then display safe/overflow states.

items.name: Responsive Card Fit Guard
items.subtitle: Preventing overflow in project cards
items.note: Editable title and description with width and line-budget controls. Shows SAFE vs OVERFLOW RISK before the UI breaks.

proof.problem: Project cards look broken when titles wrap to four lines in a two-line slot.
proof.concept: Measure text against max line budgets before shipping copy.
proof.why: UI quality control without DOM reflow — the same guard you'd use in a design system.
edgeCases: Marketing copy that grows over time
proves: Real UI quality control, not random text measurement.

items.name: Kanban Title Stress Test
items.subtitle: Layout prediction for CRM cards
items.note: Lead titles that grow until Kanban cards fail. Pretext predicts failure before the board breaks.

proof.problem: Kanban cards fail silently when lead descriptions grow past mobile widths.
proof.concept: Predict line count at card width before render.
proof.why: CRM users care about speed and clarity — overflow is a trust problem.
proves: Business-facing layout prediction for real workflow UI.

items.name: Multilingual Text Edge Cases
items.subtitle: Mixed-language wrapping
items.note: English, Spanish, Arabic, Chinese, emoji, long tokens, and code-like strings — same width, compared line counts.

proof.problem: Card copy fails differently per script — English assumptions break for CJK and RTL.
proof.concept: One prepare() path handles mixed scripts via canvas font metrics.
proof.why: Serious text measurement must handle real-world multilingual content.

items.name: Virtual Feed Height Estimator
items.subtitle: Predicting list/card height before render
items.note: Batch prepare() + layout() on dozens of fake card titles. Sum exact scroll extent with zero DOM reads.

proof.problem: Virtual lists need row heights before items enter the viewport.
proof.concept: Batch layout() across many strings — microseconds, not reflows.
proof.why: Estimated heights cause scroll jump; Pretext makes them exact.
proves: Performance thinking for feed and list UI.

items.name: Manual Line Renderer
items.subtitle: Using Pretext line output directly
items.note: layoutWithLines() materializes each line string for canvas, SVG, or custom DOM layers.

proof.problem: Some UIs need per-line strings, not just total height.
proof.concept: layoutWithLines() returns each line for manual positioning.
proof.why: Canvas, chat bubbles, and custom renderers own the paint step.
proves: Tier-2 API usage beyond height-only measurement.

items.name: Shrinkwrap Guard
items.subtitle: Tightest multiline width vs CSS fit-content
items.note: Chat-style bubbles with CSS waste visualization. Pretext binary-searches the minimum width that preserves line count — somnai pattern.

proof.problem: CSS fit-content leaves dead space on multiline bubbles and cards.
proof.concept: Binary-search the tightest width for a fixed line count without DOM reads.
proof.why: Chat UIs, captions, and tooltips need pixel-tight boxes.

items.name: Layout Shift Compare
items.subtitle: Measure first vs jump later
items.note: Side-by-side feeds: inject async content with vs without Pretext-reserved height. The non-technical proof demo.

proof.problem: Async text makes feeds and cards jump when height was unknown.
proof.concept: Reserve min-height from layout() before inserting content.
proof.why: Stable UI feels professional — visitors feel it even without reading API docs.
proves: Bridge between engineering measurement and user-visible stability.

items.name: Smoke playground
items.note: Canvas particle smoke with live parameter panel — tune motion, density, and hue.

items.name: Ambient smoke
items.note: Optional background smoke effect — off by default, user opt-in.
